summary: Iteration 21: Telegram bot integration and ePaper PDF download testing. Backend: 14/15 tests passed (93%). Telugu PDF generation fails due to fontTools library bug with Unicode range 123. English PDF download works correctly (45KB). Telegram bot routes functional. telegram_scheduler process RUNNING. Frontend ePaper page works - PDF download button visible and downloads successfully.

critical.issue: Telugu PDF returns 500 due to fontTools ValueError: expected 0 <= int <= 122, found: 123 (library bug with Telugu Unicode ranges)
critical.root_cause: fontTools 4.61.1 has a bug in subset.py _prune_post_subset() where setUnicodeRanges() rejects bit 123 which is valid for Telugu script

passed_tests: POST /api/telegram/send-pdf endpoint works (returns failed if no admin_chat_id)

telegram_bot.send_report_endpoints: Working (returns failed without admin_chat_id which is expected)

telegram_scheduler.schedule: 6 AM IST morning report, 6 PM IST evening report

epaper_pdf.english_pdf: WORKING - generates ~45KB PDF with text-only content (images removed for speed)
epaper_pdf.telugu_pdf: BROKEN - fontTools library bug with Unicode ranges
epaper_pdf.frontend_download: WORKING - PDF button visible, download triggered successfully

action_items: FIX: Telugu PDF generation - fontTools 4.61.1 bug with Unicode bit 123. Options: (1) Downgrade fontTools to <4.50, (2) Add try/except wrapper in PDF generation to skip font subsetting, (3) Use a different Telugu font
critical_code_review_comments: telegram_bot.py: BOT_TOKEN and ADMIN_CHAT_ID properly loaded from env/db
telegram_scheduler.py: Correctly runs as standalone subprocess with asyncio.run()
epaper.py PDF endpoint: Images removed (line 352: img_html = '') for faster generation - GOOD
EpaperPage.jsx: PDF download has 60s timeout - GOOD
seed_data_creation: None needed - existing articles used for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Telegram bot integration complete. telegram_scheduler running as supervisor process. English PDF download works. Telugu PDF BROKEN due to fontTools 4.61.1 library bug (ValueError: expected 0 <= int <= 122, found: 123). Main agent needs to fix Telugu PDF generation - consider downgrading fontTools or adding error handling.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: fontTools 4.61.1 subsetter raises ValueError in _prune_post_subset() when processing Telugu font glyphs. The Unicode range bit 123 is used for certain Telugu characters but fontTools.subset validates range 0-122 only
potential_fixes: Downgrade fontTools to version <4.50 where this validation didn't exist
Wrap PDF generation in try/except and use fallback for Telugu
Configure WeasyPrint to skip font subsetting: font_config.add_font_face()
